skill.name: funding-trend-forecaster
skill.description: Analyze funding abstracts and project metadata to identify topic shifts and forecast near-term grant priorities.
skill.summary: Analyze funding abstracts and project metadata to identify topic shifts and forecast near-term grant priorities.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 73 static findings were evaluated. The vast majority are false positives from pattern matching in markdown documentation (backtick code block commands flagged as shell execution, dictionary key access flagged as certificate files, and text strings flagged as weak cryptography). The only confirmed operation is file write for report export (lines 494, 498 of scripts/main.py), which is expected functionality for a report-generation tool. No credential access, no network requests, no command injection vectors, and no malicious intent were found. The code uses mock/simulated data and does not connect to external services.

low_findings.title: Filesystem write for report export
low_findings.description: The export_report method writes JSON or text output to a user-specified file path. This is expected functionality for a report-generation tool. The path is controlled by the user via the --output argument. No arbitrary or dangerous path writes detected. Dismissed as low risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown code blocks flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged backtick-delimited commands in SKILL.md and references/audit-reference.md as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All detected instances are within markdown fenced code blocks showing example usage commands. No actual shell execution occurs. Dismissed as false positive.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs flagged as network access
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged URLs in SKILL.md as network access. These are documentation references to NIH Reporter, NSF Award Search, and Horizon Europe funding portals. The actual Python code does not make any HTTP requests or use the requests library. Dismissed as false positive.

low_findings.title: Dictionary key access misidentified as certificate files
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged np.random.choice(list(topics_pool.keys())) as certificate/key file access. This is dictionary key iteration for mock data generation, not a certificate file operation. Dismissed as false positive.

low_findings.title: Text strings misidentified as weak cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged text strings in JSON metadata, argparse descriptions, YAML front matter, and markdown tables as weak cryptographic algorithm usage. No actual cryptographic operations exist in the codebase. Dismissed as false positive.

content.user_title: Forecast Research Funding Trends with NLP Analysis
content.value_statement: Researchers and grant strategists struggle to identify shifting funding priorities across major agencies. This skill analyzes funding abstracts and metadata to predict topic shifts and forecast grant priorities for the next 3 to 5 years.
actual_capabilities: Analyze funding abstracts to extract research topics, keywords, and emerging trends
Identify topic shift patterns across funding agencies like NIH, NSF, and Horizon Europe
Generate structured trend analysis reports with keyword frequency and domain growth metrics
Forecast near-term funding priorities using time-series analysis of topic distributions
Produce text or JSON formatted reports for review and sharing
Segment analysis by funding organization for comparative insights
limitations: Uses simulated data for analysis; does not connect to live funding databases or APIs
Predictions are based on trend extrapolation, not causal or econometric models
Cannot access proprietary or non-public grant data
Accuracy of forecasts decreases significantly beyond 3-year horizons

use_cases.title: Grant Portfolio Strategy
use_cases.description: A research administrator reviews past NIH and NSF awards to predict which topics will receive increased funding, helping their institution prioritize grant applications.

use_cases.title: Research Direction Planning
use_cases.description: A principal investigator analyzes funding trends to decide which research directions have the highest probability of securing grants in the next funding cycle.

use_cases.title: Policy and Investment Analysis
use_cases.description: A science policy analyst examines shifts in funding allocation across disciplines to identify strategic gaps and emerging priority areas for policy recommendations.
use_cases.target_user: Science policy analysts and research strategists

prompt_templates.title: Quick Funding Trend Overview
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the funding trend forecaster with default settings. Analyze recent funding data and show me the top 5 research topics gaining the most attention. List the growth rate for each topic.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants a fast summary of current funding trends.

prompt_templates.title: Single Agency Deep Dive
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure the forecaster to analyze only NIH data. Focus on biomedical research topics. Identify the top 3 areas where funding has increased in the past 6 months and predict their trajectory for the next 2 years.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher wants to focus on one funding agency.

prompt_templates.title: Comparative Agency Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a comparative analysis between NIH and Horizon Europe funding trends. Extract topic models for each agency separately. Show me which research areas overlap and which are unique to each agency. Generate a forecast for the next 3 years highlighting diverging trends.
prompt_templates.scenario: A grant strategist compares priorities across funding bodies.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Forecast with Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a config.json file with custom NLP parameters: set max_topics to 15, forecast confidence to 0.90, and include stop words for common grant terminology. Run the full analysis workflow with 12 months of historical data. Export the report in JSON format and identify the top 3 emerging topics with the highest growth momentum.
prompt_templates.scenario: An advanced user wants a tailored analysis with specific parameters.

output_examples.input: Show me the top growing research topics in NSF funding.
output_examples.output: The analysis of 1,542 NSF-funded projects over the past 6 months shows: Artificial Intelligence (+45% growth), Climate Technology (+32% growth), and Quantum Computing (+28% growth) are the top three areas with increasing funding attention. AI-related projects account for 23% of all awards in the period.

output_examples.input: Compare NIH and Horizon Europe research priorities.
output_examples.output: NIH and Horizon Europe show partial overlap in biotechnology and health research. NIH prioritizes clinical applications and disease-specific research, while Horizon Europe allocates more funding to foundational science and cross-border collaboration. The forecast suggests both agencies will increase investment in AI-driven health solutions over the next 3 years.

output_examples.input: What are the emerging research topics for next year?
output_examples.output: Based on trend extrapolation from recent funding data, three emerging areas for next year are: Multimodal AI applications in research, gene therapy advancements, and green hydrogen technology. Confidence in these predictions is 87% for the 1-year horizon, decreasing to 72% for the 3-year outlook.

best_practices: Define a specific time range and data source before starting analysis to focus results on relevant trends.
Review the generated metadata section first to understand the data scope and limitations of each analysis run.
Cross-reference forecast results with your domain knowledge to validate predictions against known funding announcements.
anti_patterns: Do not use the trend forecaster for investment decisions or financial trading strategies. It is designed for research planning only.
Do not treat simulated data as real funding data. The skill generates mock data for demonstration. Verify with official sources.
Do not rely on long-term forecasts beyond 3 years without additional validation. Prediction confidence decreases over time.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to live funding databases?
faq.answer: No. The skill uses simulated data to demonstrate trend analysis methods. It does not make live API calls to NIH, NSF, or Horizon Europe databases.

faq.question: How accurate are the funding predictions?
faq.answer: Predictions are based on trend extrapolation from the provided data. Short-term forecasts of 1 to 2 years are more reliable than long-term forecasts.

faq.question: What data sources does the tool support?
faq.answer: The tool is designed to work with NIH, NSF, and Horizon Europe funding data. The current implementation generates simulated data based on these sources.

faq.question: Can I add my own funding data?
faq.answer: The skill does not currently support importing external data. You can modify the mock data generation or extend the Python script to load your own datasets.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: The skill can export reports in JSON format for machine processing or plain text format for human review.

faq.question: Does this skill collect or transmit any data?
faq.answer: No. The skill processes data locally and only writes output files to the path you specify. No data is sent to external servers.
